conventions.commits: Conventional commits with GPG signing and DCO sign-off
conventions.testing: Comprehensive unit and integration tests with AAA pattern
conventions.linting: Clippy with deny warnings, rustfmt for formatting
conventions.error_handling: thiserror for libraries, anyhow for applications

patterns: Multi-language support (Rust, Python, JavaScript, Java, PHP)

related_issues.title: feat(security): evaluate tree-sitter for AST-based vulnerability detection
test(security): add negative test documenting multi-line detection limitation
test(security): document multi-line vulnerability detection limitation

constraints: Must maintain backward compatibility with existing patterns
Must support all currently supported languages
Should integrate with existing SecurityScanner architecture
test_coverage: Good coverage with integration tests and documented limitations. Current tests cover single-line vulnerabilities well but document multi-line limitations.

library_findings.notes: Core parsing library with Rust bindings
Language-specific grammar for Rust code analysis
Language-specific grammar for Python code analysis

approaches.name: Hybrid Approach (Recommended)
approaches.description: Integrate tree-sitter as an additional analysis layer alongside existing regex patterns. Use AST-based detection for multi-line vulnerabilities while keeping regex for simple patterns.
pros: Best of both worlds: regex speed + AST accuracy
Lower risk of breaking existing functionality
cons: Need to maintain two pattern systems

approaches.name: Full AST Replacement
approaches.description: Replace regex patterns entirely with tree-sitter AST queries. Convert all existing patterns to tree-sitter queries.
cons: Steeper learning curve for pattern authors

approaches.name: Fallback Approach
approaches.description: Use tree-sitter only when regex patterns fail to detect vulnerabilities. Run AST analysis as a secondary pass.
pros: Catches additional vulnerabilities without breaking existing ones
Lower performance impact for most cases
cons: May miss some vulnerabilities that could be caught by AST
Harder to maintain two detection systems

recommendation: Hybrid Approach - Integrate tree-sitter as an additional analysis layer alongside existing regex patterns. This provides the best balance of accuracy, compatibility, and maintainability while addressing the multi-line vulnerability detection limitation.
